deck_title: Pearson Skills Intelligence Engine — Learner Data Profile (Design & Definition) | Proposal Deck Plan

coverage.requirement_text: Schedule and conduct an overview and discovery meeting with Pearson during the week of 2025-12-01.
Submit any supplier questions to Pearson by 2025-12-08 5pm EST and incorporate Pearson answers returned on 2025-12-12.
Submit the final proposal by 2025-12-19 5pm EST to samir.aragon@pearson.com and the Pearson Client Partner.

Obtain Pearson’s specific written approval before using any AI-powered tools to create or aid creation of any content delivered under the SOW; AI-assisted content without approval will not be accepted or paid.
Define a canonical, secure, interoperable Learner Data Profile supporting ingress from Pearson/partner systems and egress to consuming applications, including data exchange patterns.

Deliver M1: Data Requirements & Inventory—catalogue domains (skills, credentials, occupation/ontology mappings, language proficiency, inferred skills, identity/employment, consent/provenance) and map sources of truth; obtain approval.

Deliver M2: Data Model & Schema—produce conceptual ERD, logical model, physical storage design, and canonical JSON/Avro/Parquet schemas with versioning and data contracts; signed-off and stored in a repo.
Deliver M3: Ingress Architecture blueprint for batch and streaming ingestion including connectors (API/MCP), CDC/event patterns, retries/DLQ, and identity resolution; obtain approval.

Deliver M4: Egress Architecture/interfaces including read models and delivery interfaces (REST/GraphQL APIs/MCPs), event topics for deltas, and bulk exports; provide OpenAPI/GraphQL specs and sample payloads.
Provide an Azure-first reference architecture and technology stack recommendation (data lake/warehouse, processing, APIs, governance, observability, CI/CD).
Define and document non-functional requirements including performance, availability, scalability, cost guardrails, data quality SLAs, observability, lineage, and explainability.
Provide rapid implementation paths (staged options such as MVP, partner-source extension, scale & hardening) and deliver M6: Implementation Plan & Readout including handover and demo.
Provide project management and knowledge transfer: Agile ceremonies, documentation, training, weekly reporting to Pearson Product/Engineering leads.
Meet acceptance criteria: M1–M6 approved by Product, Security, Data Governance, and Architecture reviewers; publish ADRs, API specs, schema registry entries, and runbooks to Pearson repositories.
Support data quality acceptance: achieve ≥95% coverage for mandatory fields in MVP sources and ensure automated data-quality validation is in place (design/definition).
Ensure egress APIs/feeds are validated by at least one consuming app using sample data.

Design security/privacy/compliance to Pearson policies and align to ISO 27001/27701 and SOC 2 Type II equivalents; include classification/labeling, RBAC, audit logging, secrets in Key Vault; use Entra ID least privilege with PIM/JIT; support audit/lineage via Purview/Monitor; include ethical AI controls and artifacts (threat model, DPIA as required, control mapping, logging/monitoring plan).
Exclude out-of-scope work: no implementation/build-out, no end-user UI build-out, and no enterprise licensing/procurement for third-party tools.

Provide proposal contents: experience/case studies/references; technical approach; project plan/timeline; team composition; outcome-based pricing by milestone; risk management; value-added services; transition approach; recommended SLAs/KPIs; and proposal assumptions.
